items.title: Why the QR Code Was Invented to Track Car Parts
items.description: You scan one to pay at a sari-sari store, pull up a restaurant menu, or board a flight. The QR code has quietly become one of the most universal pieces of interface design on the planet. But it was never meant for any of that. "AI that reads sign language" sounds like a research lab and a GPU cluster. But a genuinely useful starting version runs entirely in your browser, with no model upload and no cloud — the camera feed never leaves your machine. Here's how I built a webcam sign reader from scratch.

items.title: What a Neural Net Actually Does — the Intuition, No Math
items.description:
People say a neural network "learns to see" or "understands images," and it sounds like sci-fi. It isn't. A neural net does something much more mechanical — and once you see the shape of it, the mystery evaporates. No math in this one, just the intuition.

The JSON became readable.

The indentation looked clean.

items.title: Why JSON Formatting Isn't Enough for Debugging APIs
items.description:

You've probably done it too.

items.title: offset-path

items.title: @function
items.description: The @function at-rule defines CSS custom functions.
